decisions.summary: Adopt Option A (auto-refresh) over Option B (remove the rule). The graph summary is genuinely useful for orientation; the failure is enforcement, not value. Fix the enforcement.

Refresh runs in postMergeLifecycle (src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts), not as a PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write. Post-merge is the cheapest enforcement point — one location, runs out-of-band, naturally serialized per issue, keeps the graph at most one merge stale.

Extract refresh into src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts with a single async entry point refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId). The merge-agent imports and invokes it. Rationale: isolates the git+graphify shell sequence for unit testing and keeps merge-agent.ts focused on lifecycle orchestration.

Refresh runs against projectPath (the project's main checkout), not the workspace. postMergeLifecycle already passes projectPath; workspace graphify-out/ is stale-and-doomed at close-out time, so refreshing it would be wasted work.
Skip silently (info-level log, never throw) when graphify is not on PATH or graphify-out/ does not exist in projectPath. Projects without graphify must not have their merges fail or warn.

Commit message: chore(graphify): refresh after <ISSUE-ID>. Push directly to origin/main with one fetch+rebase retry on non-fast-forward. Failure to push is logged but does not fail postMergeLifecycle — the doctor check is the safety net.

Add a pan doctor check (graphify-stale) that warns when graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime is older than the latest origin/main commit. ok when fresh, warn when stale, skipped (no check) when graphify-out/ is missing. This catches situations where the auto-refresh silently failed.

CLAUDE.md keeps the 'read GRAPH_SUMMARY.md on task start' guidance (it is now reliably fresh) and DROPS the 'After modifying code, run graphify update .' line (auto-enforced). Add one sentence noting the merge-agent refresh.

All shell-out from refresh.ts uses execAsync (promisified exec) — no execSync. Required because the refresh runs inside the dashboard server process post-restart, which must not block the event loop. Repo rule: src/.claude/rules/no-execsync-server.md.

Refresh runs between transitionIssueToVerifyingOnMain and compactBeads in postMergeLifecycle. Rationale: the issue label has just been transitioned (so any failure attaches to a verifying-on-main issue cleanly), and the rest of cleanup proceeds independently if refresh skips/fails.

hazards.summary: Concurrent postMergeLifecycle runs from different issues could race on git push origin main (non-fast-forward).
hazards.mitigation: After a failed push, refreshGraphify runs git fetch origin main + git pull --rebase origin main + git push once. If the retry also fails, log a warning via logActivity and return — the next merge will refresh again.

hazards.summary: Husky commit-msg hook rejects package.json version changes unless the subject is 'chore: release X.Y.Z'. Our commit subject is 'chore(graphify): refresh after PAN-XXXX' and only touches graphify-out/, so the hook should pass — but a future graphify version that writes outside graphify-out/ could trip it.
hazards.mitigation: refreshGraphify explicitly stages ONLY graphify-out/ (git add graphify-out/), never git add -A. If git diff --cached --quiet returns 0 (no changes), skip the commit entirely.

hazards.summary: graphify update on a very large repo could exceed the 10s budget in the acceptance criteria.
hazards.mitigation: Wrap the graphify update invocation in execAsync with a 60s timeout (generous safety net). On timeout, log a warning and skip the commit. The test bead captures wall-clock time on this repo to validate <10s in practice.

hazards.summary: postMergeLifecycle runs in the post-restart server process from a detached deploy script — running graphify commands from inside this process must not interfere with the restart sequence.
hazards.mitigation: refreshGraphify is awaited inline within the existing postMergeLifecycle promise chain (which already awaits other async steps like compactBeads). No new processes spawned beyond the graphify exec itself.

hazards.summary: If graphify-out/ is in .gitignore for some downstream project that uses Panopticon, our `git add graphify-out/` would silently no-op and the commit/push would skip — but the message logged might claim success.
hazards.mitigation: After git add, check git diff --cached --quiet. If no staged changes, log 'graphify-out/ has no changes to commit (possibly gitignored)' and return without erroring.

hazards.summary: The doctor check compares GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime to origin/main HEAD commit time, but local clones may have a fetched-but-not-pulled main that is ahead of working tree. False positives are possible.
hazards.mitigation: Use the timestamp of the most recent commit on the currently checked-out HEAD when comparing — not origin/main HEAD. If GRAPH_SUMMARY.md is newer than HEAD, it is fresh by definition.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1408: Graphify graph drifts because no automation enforces updates
Auto-mode planning complete. Adopted Option A (auto-refresh) via postMergeLifecycle hook + pan doctor staleness check. 5 beads decomposed.
